data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0015/sessions/0018-horizon-T14

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
Useful context for the next Horizon session (T14 / Hilbert lane):

- Relevant files: `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SerreFiniteness.lean` holds the sole open leaf `sectionGradedModule_fg` (`:64`, Serre finiteness on ℙⁿ; no Mathlib substrate for coherent-sheaf cohomology in v4.31) plus the now-landed axiom-clean fibre connectors (`IsProjectiveWith.fiber`, `fiberModule_isFinitePresentation`) and their `sorryAx`-inheriting corollaries (`sectionGradedModule_fg_fiber`, `hilbertFunction_finiteDimensional`). Companion files: `HilbertPolynomial.lean`, `ProjectiveMorphism.lean`, `SerreTwist.lean`, `GradedHilbertSerre.lean`.

- The I-0109 Hilbert-lane bridge is the remaining gap: `gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational` (`GradedHilbertSerre.lean`) wants `M` finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin d+1) κ(s)` via degree-1 coordinate endos, whereas the leaf's conclusion-2 gives only finiteness over the abstract section ring `R(X_s,L_s)` — a form mismatch needing `Γ(ℙ^d,O(1))` coordinate-ring infrastructure. Memory `serre-finiteness-leaf-decomposition` and `I-0109` carry the precise shape and the R1–R4 program.

- Blueprint context: `sec:projective_vocabulary` / `sec:quot_representable` in `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`; the four new fibre nodes (`lem:projective_with_fiber`, `lem:fiberModule_finitePresentation`, `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg_fiber`, `lem:hilbertFunction_finiteDimensional`) sit after `lem:projective_with_base_change`. Blueprint pinning is honest: pure transports are proof-`\leanok`, the two fibre-Serre corollaries are statement-`\leanok` only (they inherit the leaf's `sorry`). Reference `\source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}` under `references/`.

- Build/env note: full AJC cone builds green (8606-job replay observed this session, exit 0); the two named `sorry` warnings are `SerreFiniteness.lean:64` (the intended leaf) and `QuotFunctorDef.lean:1073` (the I-0118 `QuotScheme` restatement — properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem; still open, out of T14 scope).

# Task focus
Task: T14
Title: Ampleness / projective-morphism / Serre-finiteness foundation (unblocks Picard representability)
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/*.lean,MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex

Build the ample / very-ample line-bundle and projective-morphism foundation that Mathlib v4.31 lacks. This is the SINGLE shared prerequisite blocking QuotScheme representability, curve projectivity, and the Picard-scheme root instHasPicScheme (see inbox I-0109 ampleness gap and I-0118 statement trap). It has NO prerequisite and unblocks T15. Do NOT touch the FGA assembly or attempt QuotScheme representability here — build only the foundation.

MATHLIB v4.31 ASSETS TO BUILD ON (verified present): AlgebraicGeometry.Proj (ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme.lean); Proj.toSpecZero with instance IsProper for finite-type graded algebras (ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean:368), LocallyOfFiniteType (:136), IsSeparated (:82); Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:439) and Proj.map functoriality (Functor.lean:144); MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule / MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra (RingTheory/MvPolynomial/Homogeneous.lean:608); AffineSpace pattern (AlgebraicGeometry/AffineSpace.lean:44); SheafOfModules.unit. Verified ABSENT (must be built): any IsProjective/QuasiProjective morphism class, VeryAmple/IsAmple for sheaves, relative Proj / P^n_S over a base, Serre twisting sheaf O(1).

DELIVERABLES (new sorry-free Lean files or honestly named-leaf-factored, each with a complete-proof blueprint node):
1. Relative projective space P(n;S) := pullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from (Proj (MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin (n+1)) Int))), in the AffineSpace.lean:44 style; derive proper + locallyOfFiniteType + separated structural morphism by base change from the Mathlib Proj instances.
2. Serre twist O(1) (and O(m)) as a SheafOfModules on Proj 𝒜, glued over the awayι / basic-open affine cover with transition t_i/t_j — REUSE the project module-gluing engine (Picard/GlueDescent.lean, GrassmannianZariskiSheaf.lean, Scheme.Modules.glue).
3. A projective-with-L predicate: pi : X ⟶ S is projective carrying L when there is a closed immersion i : X ↪ P(n;S) over S with L ≅ i^* O(1). Prove stability facts the endgame consumes: base change, composition with closed immersions, and that it implies IsProper.
4. Serre finiteness / boundedness: for pi proper with L relatively ample, the section graded module ⊕_m Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^⊗m) is finitely generated over ⊕_m Γ(X_s, L_s^⊗m) (blueprint lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, I-0109, the deep remaining Hilbert-lane input) and the uniform Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity bound (blueprint lem:quot_boundedness). The DirectSum-decomposition bridge to gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational (already sorry-free) is documented in I-0109.

Verify each file with lake build (or lake env lean) + lean_verify axiom checks. Prefer axiom-clean; where a step is genuinely too deep, factor a NAMED typed sorry with an honest statement + blueprint node. Coordinate blueprint nodes with the existing Picard_QuotScheme.tex / Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex chapters.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [done/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Progress` and `## Why I stopped`. In `## Progress`, use only inline `-` bullets, one per relevant file or target, e.g. `- FileA.lean: 4 sorries -> 3 sorries; closed the base case.` or `- FileB.lean: No change because dependency X is blocked.` Do not use nested bullets or wrapped multi-line bullets there. In `## Why I stopped`, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.ProjectiveMorphism
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.HilbertPolynomial
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.PullbackFinitePresentation
10	
11	/-!
12	# Serre finiteness for the section graded module (statement)
13	
14	The deep remaining input of the Hilbert-polynomial lane
15	(`lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`, inbox `I-0109`): for a projective scheme over
16	a field carrying a very ample line bundle `L` and a coherent sheaf `F`, the
17	section graded ring `R(X, L) = ⊕ₘ Γ(X, L^{⊗m})` is Noetherian, the section
18	graded module `M(X, F, L) = ⊕ₘ Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite module over it,
19	and each graded component is a finite-dimensional `κ`-vector space.
20	
21	This file gives the **honest statement** against the projectivity vocabulary
22	of `Picard/ProjectiveMorphism.lean` (the `IsProjectiveWith` closed-immersion
23	predicate — very ampleness in the encoding settled in inbox `I-0118`), as a
24	named typed `sorry` leaf: the proof is Serre's theorem ([Hartshorne] II.5.17
25	ff., [Nitsure] §1) and is genuinely deep (multi-session; it needs the
26	pushforward-to-projective-space reduction and cohomological finiteness on
27	`ℙⁿ`).
28	
29	Compared with the blueprint node, which hypothesises an *ample* `L` and
30	passes to a Veronese power, the Lean statement hypothesises the very ample
31	form directly (`IsProjectiveWith`) — this is the form in which the
32	Quot-scheme endgame ([Nitsure] §5) consumes it.
33	
34	Blueprint: `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`
35	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
36	-/
37	
38	open CategoryTheory Limits
39	
40	noncomputable section
41	
42	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
43	
44	/-- The structural `κ`-algebra structure on the global sections of a scheme
45	over `Spec κ` (the absolute analogue of `Scheme.Hom.fiberResidueMap`). -/
46	def Scheme.Hom.structuralSectionsHom {X : Scheme.{0}} {κ : Type} [CommRing κ]
47	    (π : X ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of κ)) : κ →+* Γ(X, ⊤) :=
48	  ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of κ)).inv ≫ π.appTop).hom
49	
50	/-- **Serre finiteness for the section graded module**
51	(`lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`; [Nitsure] §1, [Hartshorne] II.5.17 ff.):
52	for `π : X ⟶ Spec κ` projective carrying the very ample line bundle `L`
53	and a coherent (finitely presented) sheaf `F` on `X`,
54	
55	1. the section graded ring `⊕ₘ Γ(X, L^{⊗m})` is Noetherian;
56	2. the section graded module `⊕ₘ Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite module
57	   over it;
58	3. each component `Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite-dimensional `κ`-vector
59	   space (via the structural `κ`-action).
60	
61	NAMED LEAF (`sorry`): the geometric heart of the Hilbert-polynomial lane
62	(inbox `I-0109`), awaiting the pushforward-to-`ℙⁿ` reduction and
63	cohomological finiteness of coherent sheaves on projective space. -/
64	theorem sectionGradedModule_fg {X : Scheme.{0}} {κ : Type} [Field κ]
65	    (π : X ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of κ)) (L F : X.Modules)
66	    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
67	    (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring L
68	     IsNoetherianRing (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg L)))
69	    ∧ (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring L
70	       letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedModule_gmodule F L
71	       Module.Finite (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg L))
72	         (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L)))
73	    ∧ ∀ m : ℕ,
74	        letI := Module.compHom (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L m)
75	          π.structuralSectionsHom
76	        FiniteDimensional κ (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L m) := by
77	  sorry
78	
79	/-! ## Descent to scheme-theoretic fibres — the Hilbert-lane connector
80	
81	The absolute leaf `sectionGradedModule_fg` is stated over a *field*; the Hilbert
82	polynomial `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` and its graded Hilbert function
83	`Scheme.hilbertFunction` (`Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean`) live at the
84	scheme-theoretic fibre `X_s = π.fiber s` over the residue field `κ(s)`, which is
85	a field.  The lemmas below are the sorry-free geometric glue that transports
86	`IsProjectiveWith` and finite presentation to a fibre (both via the already
87	proved `IsProjectiveWith.baseChange` and `Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation`),
88	so the deep Serre leaf applies fibrewise; the corollary
89	`hilbertFunction_finiteDimensional` then shows the graded Hilbert function is a
90	genuine (finite) dimension for projective fibres — the finiteness half of the
91	bridge to `AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`
92	(inbox `I-0109`).  The remaining step — packaging finite generation as the
93	`MvPolynomial`-finiteness input of that engine — is documented there. -/
94	
95	variable {X S : Scheme.{0}}
96	
97	/-- **`IsProjectiveWith` descends to scheme-theoretic fibres**: if `π : X ⟶ S`
98	is projective carrying `L`, then the structural morphism of the fibre
99	`X_s = π.fiber s`, namely `π.fiberToSpecResidueField s : X_s ⟶ Spec κ(s)`, is
100	projective carrying the restriction `L_s = π.fiberModule s L`.  Immediate from
101	`IsProjectiveWith.baseChange` along `S.fromSpecResidueField s`, since the fibre
102	is the base change of `π` along that morphism (`Scheme.Hom.fiber`). -/
103	theorem Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.fiber {π : X ⟶ S} {L : X.Modules}
104	    (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (s : S) :
105	    (π.fiberToSpecResidueField s).IsProjectiveWith (π.fiberModule s L) :=
106	  h.baseChange (S.fromSpecResidueField s)
107	
108	/-- **Finite presentation descends to scheme-theoretic fibres**: the restriction
109	`F_s = π.fiberModule s F` of a finitely presented sheaf `F` to the fibre is
110	finitely presented (pullback of a finitely presented sheaf along the fibre
111	embedding `π.fiberι s`). -/
112	theorem Scheme.Hom.fiberModule_isFinitePresentation (π : X ⟶ S) (s : S)
113	    {F : X.Modules} (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
114	    (π.fiberModule s F).IsFinitePresentation :=
115	  Scheme.Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation (π.fiberι s) F hF
116	
117	/-- **Serre finiteness along a fibre** (`lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` specialised
118	to the fibre over `s`): for `π : X ⟶ S` projective carrying `L` and `F` finitely
119	presented, the section graded ring of the fibre restriction `L_s` is Noetherian,
120	and the section graded module of `F_s` is finite over it.  This is the deep leaf
121	`sectionGradedModule_fg` applied to the fibre morphism via
122	`IsProjectiveWith.fiber` and `fiberModule_isFinitePresentation`; it inherits the
123	single named `sorry` of that leaf and no other.  The finite-dimensionality of
124	each graded component — the third conclusion of `sectionGradedModule_fg`,
125	matching the graded Hilbert function — is recorded separately as
126	`hilbertFunction_finiteDimensional` (stated with the `Scheme.hilbertFunction`
127	residue-field module structure). -/
128	theorem sectionGradedModule_fg_fiber (π : X ⟶ S) (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
129	    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
130	    (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring (π.fiberModule s L)
131	     IsNoetherianRing (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg (π.fiberModule s L))))
132	    ∧ (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring (π.fiberModule s L)
133	       letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedModule_gmodule
134	         (π.fiberModule s F) (π.fiberModule s L)
135	       Module.Finite (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg (π.fiberModule s L)))
136	         (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg
137	           (π.fiberModule s F) (π.fiberModule s L)))) :=
138	  ⟨(sectionGradedModule_fg (π.fiberToSpecResidueField s) (π.fiberModule s L)
139	      (π.fiberModule s F) (hproj.fiber s)
140	      (Scheme.Hom.fiberModule_isFinitePresentation π s hF)).1,
141	   (sectionGradedModule_fg (π.fiberToSpecResidueField s) (π.fiberModule s L)
142	      (π.fiberModule s F) (hproj.fiber s)
143	      (Scheme.Hom.fiberModule_isFinitePresentation π s hF)).2.1⟩
144	
145	/-- **The graded Hilbert function is a genuine (finite) dimension for projective
146	fibres**: for `π : X ⟶ S` projective carrying `L` and `F` finitely presented,
147	each twisted-section space `Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` whose `κ(s)`-dimension is
148	`Scheme.hilbertFunction π L F s m` is finite-dimensional — so the graded Hilbert
149	function is not merely the `Module.finrank` junk value.  This is the
150	finite-dimensionality half of the input to the graded Hilbert–Serre rationality
151	engine (`AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`, inbox
152	`I-0109`). -/

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SectionGradedRing
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GradedHilbertSerre
9	
10	/-!
11	# The Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf along a fibre (Nitsure §1)
12	
13	This file gives the **real definition** of the Hilbert polynomial
14	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` of a sheaf of modules `F` on
15	`X ⟶ S` at a point `s : S` relative to a line bundle `L`, via the graded
16	Hilbert function of the fibre section module — the `H⁰`-only encoding pinned
17	in the blueprint (`def:hilbert_polynomial`,
18	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`):
19	
20	* the fibre `X_s = π.fiber s` is Mathlib's scheme-theoretic fibre
21	  `pullback π (S.fromSpecResidueField s)`;
22	* the restrictions `F_s`, `L_s` are the module pullbacks along the fibre
23	  embedding `π.fiberι s` (`Scheme.Hom.fiberModule`);
24	* the twists `F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m}` are `Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow` of
25	  `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SectionGradedRing`;
26	* their global sections are `κ(s)`-vector spaces via the structural ring
27	  homomorphism `κ(s) →+* Γ(X_s, 𝒪)` (`Scheme.Hom.fiberResidueMap`,
28	  `Scheme.Hom.fiberSectionsModule`), giving the graded Hilbert function
29	  `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`);
30	* `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial π L F s` is the unique `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]` that agrees
31	  with the graded Hilbert function for all `m ≫ 0` when such a polynomial
32	  exists, and the junk value `0` otherwise.  Whenever the eventual-match
33	  condition holds the polynomial is unique (two rational polynomials agreeing
34	  at all large naturals are equal), so the classical-choice extraction is
35	  canonical; no numerical invariant is lost (see the Serre-vanishing
36	  discussion in `def:hilbert_polynomial`).
37	
38	The bridge from the graded Hilbert–Serre rationality engine is
39	`Scheme.existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb`: when the graded Hilbert
40	function is a rational Hilbert function (`AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb`, the
41	output shape of `AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`), the
42	eventual-match condition holds, the matching polynomial is unique, and
43	`hilbertPolynomial` computes it (`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_hilbertPoly`).
44	Producing the `IsRatHilb` hypothesis for proper `X_s` and ample `L_s` is the
45	finite-generation theorem `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` (Serre), which is
46	future work; it enters only through the lemmas here, never through the
47	definition of `hilbertPolynomial` itself.
48	
49	Blueprint: `def:hilbert_polynomial`, `sec:graded_hilbert_polynomial`
50	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
51	Source: [Nitsure], §1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert Polynomials"
52	(`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex`, L453–L478);
53	cf. [Hartshorne], III.5.2.
54	-/
55	
56	set_option autoImplicit false
57	
58	universe u
59	
60	open CategoryTheory Limits
61	
62	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
63	
64	namespace Scheme
65	
66	variable {S X : Scheme.{u}}
67	
68	/-- The restriction `F_s = F|_{X_s}` of a sheaf of modules `F` on `X` to the
69	scheme-theoretic fibre `X_s = π.fiber s` of `π : X ⟶ S` over `s : S`: the
70	module pullback along the fibre embedding `π.fiberι s : π.fiber s ⟶ X`.
71	Blueprint: the restriction `F_s` of `def:hilbert_polynomial`. -/
72	noncomputable def Hom.fiberModule (π : X ⟶ S) (s : S) (F : X.Modules) :
73	    (π.fiber s).Modules :=
74	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (π.fiberι s)).obj F
75	
76	/-- The structural ring homomorphism `κ(s) ⟶ Γ(X_s, 𝒪_{X_s})` of the fibre
77	`X_s = π.fiber s`: global sections of the structural morphism
78	`π.fiberToSpecResidueField s : X_s ⟶ Spec κ(s)`, transported along the
79	canonical isomorphism `Γ(Spec κ(s), ⊤) ≅ κ(s)`.  It makes every section
80	module of a sheaf of modules on the fibre a `κ(s)`-vector space
81	(`Scheme.Hom.fiberSectionsModule`). -/
82	noncomputable def Hom.fiberResidueMap (π : X ⟶ S) (s : S) :
83	    S.residueField s ⟶ Γ(π.fiber s, ⊤) :=
84	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (S.residueField s)).inv ≫ (π.fiberToSpecResidueField s).appTop
85	
86	/-- The `κ(s)`-module structure on the global sections of a sheaf of modules
87	`G` on the fibre `X_s = π.fiber s`: restriction of scalars along the
88	structural ring homomorphism `κ(s) →+* Γ(X_s, 𝒪)`
89	(`Scheme.Hom.fiberResidueMap`).  Not an instance (the fibre presentation of
90	the scheme is not canonical); brought into scope with `letI` at use sites. -/
91	@[reducible] noncomputable def Hom.fiberSectionsModule (π : X ⟶ S) (s : S)
92	    (G : (π.fiber s).Modules) :
93	    Module (S.residueField s) Γ(G, ⊤) :=
94	  Module.compHom Γ(G, ⊤) (π.fiberResidueMap s).hom
95	
96	/-- The **graded Hilbert function** of `F` at the fibre over `s : S`, relative
97	to the line bundle `L`:
98	
99	`m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`,
100	
101	the dimension over the residue field `κ(s)` of the global sections of the
102	`m`-th twist of the restriction `F_s` by `L_s` on the scheme-theoretic fibre
103	`X_s = π.fiber s`.  This is the graded Hilbert function of the section graded
104	module `M(X_s, F_s, L_s)` (`def:sectionGradedModule`); it uses only `H⁰`.
105	When the dimension is not finite, `Module.finrank` takes the junk value `0`.
106	Blueprint: the graded Hilbert function of `def:hilbert_polynomial`. -/
107	noncomputable def hilbertFunction (π : X ⟶ S) (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
108	    (m : ℕ) : ℕ :=
109	  letI := π.fiberSectionsModule s
110	    (Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow (π.fiberModule s F) (π.fiberModule s L) m)
111	  Module.finrank (S.residueField s)
112	    Γ(Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow (π.fiberModule s F) (π.fiberModule s L) m, ⊤)
113	
114	/-- Two rational polynomials that agree at all sufficiently large natural
115	arguments are equal: the agreement set is infinite, so the difference has
116	infinitely many roots. -/
117	private lemma eq_of_eval_nat_eventually_eq {Φ Ψ : Polynomial ℚ} {N₁ N₂ : ℕ}
118	    {f : ℕ → ℚ} (h₁ : ∀ m : ℕ, N₁ < m → Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = f m)
119	    (h₂ : ∀ m : ℕ, N₂ < m → Ψ.eval (m : ℚ) = f m) : Φ = Ψ := by
120	  refine Polynomial.eq_of_infinite_eval_eq _ _ ?_
121	  apply ((Set.Ioi_infinite (max N₁ N₂)).image
122	    (Nat.cast_injective (R := ℚ)).injOn).mono
123	  rintro x ⟨n, hn, rfl⟩
124	  rw [Set.mem_Ioi, sup_lt_iff] at hn
125	  exact Set.mem_setOf_eq ▸ (h₁ n hn.1).trans (h₂ n hn.2).symm
126	
127	open Classical in
128	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a sheaf of modules `F` on `X` over `S` at
129	the fibre over `s ∈ S`, relative to the line bundle `L`
130	(`def:hilbert_polynomial`, [Nitsure] §1; cf. [Hartshorne] III.5.2): the
131	unique polynomial `Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` that agrees, for all `m ≫ 0`, with the
132	graded Hilbert function
133	
134	`m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
135	
136	of the fibre section module (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`), when such a
137	polynomial exists — and the junk value `0` otherwise.  Uniqueness holds
138	unconditionally (two polynomials agreeing at all large naturals are equal,
139	`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually`), so the extraction is canonical.
140	
141	For proper `X_s`, ample `L_s` and coherent `F_s` the existence condition
142	holds (graded Hilbert–Serre; the `IsRatHilb`-conditional form is
143	`Scheme.existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb`, and the remaining input
144	is the finite generation `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`), and for `m ≫ 0` the
145	graded Hilbert function agrees with the Euler characteristic
146	`χ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` by Serre vanishing, so `Φ_{F,s}` is the classical
147	(Snapper) Hilbert polynomial — no numerical invariant is lost.  When `F` is
148	`S`-flat the function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`. -/
149	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S)
150	    [LocallyOfFiniteType π] (L F : X.Modules) (s : S) :
151	    Polynomial ℚ :=
152	  if h : ∃ Φ : Polynomial ℚ, ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
153	      Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)
154	  then h.choose else 0
155	
156	section HilbertPolynomialApi
157	
158	variable [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π]
159	variable (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
160	
161	/-- **Well-definedness of the Hilbert polynomial**: any polynomial that agrees
162	with the graded Hilbert function `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` for
163	all `m ≫ 0` *is* `hilbertPolynomial π L F s`.  This is the uniqueness clause
164	of `def:hilbert_polynomial`. -/
165	theorem hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually {Φ : Polynomial ℚ} {N : ℕ}
166	    (hΦ : ∀ m : ℕ, N < m → Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) :
167	    hilbertPolynomial π L F s = Φ := by
168	  have h : ∃ Φ' : Polynomial ℚ, ∃ N' : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N' < m →
169	      Φ'.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) := ⟨Φ, N, hΦ⟩
170	  rw [hilbertPolynomial, dif_pos h]
171	  obtain ⟨N', hN'⟩ := h.choose_spec
172	  exact eq_of_eval_nat_eventually_eq hN' hΦ
173	
174	/-- **Defining property of the Hilbert polynomial**: if some polynomial
175	agrees with the graded Hilbert function for all `m ≫ 0`, then
176	`hilbertPolynomial π L F s` does. -/
177	theorem hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually
178	    (h : ∃ Φ : Polynomial ℚ, ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
179	      Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) :
180	    ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
181	      (hilbertPolynomial π L F s).eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) := by
182	  obtain ⟨Φ, N, hΦ⟩ := h
183	  refine ⟨N, fun m hm => ?_⟩
184	  rw [hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually π L F s hΦ]
185	  exact hΦ m hm
186	
187	open PowerSeries in
188	omit [IsLocallyNoetherian S] [LocallyOfFiniteType π] in
189	/-- **Existence and uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial from rationality of
190	the Hilbert series** (the polynomial-extraction step of
191	`thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule`): if the graded Hilbert function of `F` at
192	`s` is a rational Hilbert function of some order `d`
193	(`AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb`, the output of the graded Hilbert–Serre engine
194	`AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`), then there is a
195	*unique* polynomial agreeing with it for all `m ≫ 0`.  Combines the
196	rationality datum with Mathlib's `Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly`
197	coefficient extraction (`lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib`). -/
198	theorem existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb {d : ℕ}
199	    (hrat : IsRatHilb (fun m => (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) d) :
200	    ∃! Φ : Polynomial ℚ, ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
201	      Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) := by
202	  obtain ⟨p, N, hp⟩ := hrat
203	  refine ⟨Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d, ⟨max N p.natDegree, fun m hm => ?_⟩, ?_⟩
204	  · rw [← Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d
205	      (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_right _ _) hm)]
206	    exact (hp m (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_left _ _) hm)).symm
207	  · rintro Ψ ⟨N', hΨ⟩
208	    refine eq_of_eval_nat_eventually_eq hΨ
209	      (f := fun m => (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) (N₂ := max N p.natDegree)
210	      (fun m hm => ?_)
211	    rw [← Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d
212	      (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_right _ _) hm)]
213	    exact (hp m (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_left _ _) hm)).symm
214	
215	open PowerSeries in
216	/-- Under the rationality hypothesis, `hilbertPolynomial π L F s` satisfies
217	the defining eventual-agreement property with the graded Hilbert function.
218	This is the conditional content of `thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule`; the
219	geometric input producing `IsRatHilb` (properness of `X_s` + ampleness of
220	`L_s` via `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`) is future work. -/
221	theorem hilbertPolynomial_eval_of_isRatHilb {d : ℕ}
222	    (hrat : IsRatHilb (fun m => (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) d) :
223	    ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
224	      (hilbertPolynomial π L F s).eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) :=
225	  hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually π L F s
226	    (existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb π L F s hrat).exists
227	
228	open PowerSeries in
229	/-- The Hilbert polynomial computes as Mathlib's `Polynomial.hilbertPoly`
230	numerator-extraction applied to any rational-series presentation of the graded
231	Hilbert function. -/
232	theorem hilbertPolynomial_eq_hilbertPoly {p : Polynomial ℚ} {d N : ℕ}
233	    (hp : ∀ m : ℕ, N < m → (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) =
234	      ((p : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ d).val).coeff m) :
235	    hilbertPolynomial π L F s = Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d := by
236	  refine hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually π L F s
237	    (N := max N p.natDegree) (fun m hm => ?_)
238	  rw [← Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d
239	    (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_right _ _) hm)]
240	  exact (hp m (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_left _ _) hm)).symm
241	
242	end HilbertPolynomialApi
243	
244	end Scheme
245	
246	end AlgebraicGeometry

231	/-- **G1(a) — independence of the graded pieces of a homogeneous submodule.** The graded
232	pieces `ℳ i ⊓ p` of any submodule `p` are independent, since they refine the independent
233	grading family `ℳ` of the ambient module. Project-local: half of the induced internal
234	direct sum decomposition of a homogeneous submodule. -/
235	theorem homogeneousSubmodule_inf_iSupIndep (p : Submodule R M) :
236	    iSupIndep fun i => ℳ i ⊓ p :=

238	
239	/-- **G1(b) — a homogeneous submodule is the supremum of its graded pieces.** For an
240	internally graded module `M = ⨁ ℳ i` and a homogeneous submodule `p`
241	(`Submodule.IsHomogeneous`), `p = ⨆ i, (ℳ i ⊓ p)`. Combined with
242	`homogeneousSubmodule_inf_iSupIndep` this exhibits the induced internal direct sum grading
243	`p = ⨁ i, (ℳ i ⊓ p)` that Mathlib's `HomogeneousSubmodule` scaffold does not supply; it
244	gives the graded kernel `K = ker(x : M → M(1))` its grading
245	`K_n = ker(x : M_n → M_{n+1})`.
246	
247	Stated in the ambient `M` (independence + supremum) rather than as
248	`DirectSum.IsInternal` on the subtype `↥p`: the latter triggers a runaway `isDefEq`
249	reduction of `DirectSum.coeLinearMap` over a subtype module. Project-local: the homogeneity

268	degreewise with `φ = (x : M_n → M_{n+1})` this is the `hdiff` hypothesis consumed by

634	    (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) • m = t i m := by
635	  change polyEndHom t hcomm (MvPolynomial.X i) • m = t i m
636	  rw [polyEndHom_X, Module.End.smul_def]
637	
638	/-- In the polynomial-module structure, a constant `C c` acts by the scalar `c`. -/
639	lemma polyModule_C_smul {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
640	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) (c : κ) (m : M) :
641	    letI := polyModule t hcomm
642	    (MvPolynomial.C c : MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) • m = c • m := by
643	  change polyEndHom t hcomm (MvPolynomial.C c) • m = c • m
644	  rw [polyEndHom_C, Module.End.smul_def]
645	  simp
646	
647	/-- The polynomial-module structure is compatible with the `κ`-action (scalar tower):
648	the algebra map `κ → MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` followed by the polynomial action recovers the
649	original `κ`-action on `M`. -/
650	lemma polyModule_isScalarTower {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
651	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) :
652	    letI := polyModule t hcomm
653	    IsScalarTower κ (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) M := by
654	  letI := polyModule t hcomm
655	  refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun c m => ?_
656	  change polyEndHom t hcomm (algebraMap κ (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) c) • m = c • m
657	  rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, polyEndHom_C, Module.End.smul_def]
658	  simp
659

/-! ## Engine-ready packaging and the rationality of the Hilbert function

The graded Hilbert–Serre rationality engine
`AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`
(`Picard/GradedHilbertSerre.lean`, sorry-free) turns an abstract finitely
generated `ℕ`-graded `κ`-vector space — one carrying `r` commuting degree-one
endomorphisms and finite over the polynomial ring `κ[X₀,…,X_{r-1}]` — into a
rational Hilbert function (`AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb`).  To feed the geometric
fibre section module `⊕ₘ Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` into that engine we package its
input as the structure `GradedHilbert` and factor Serre finiteness **in
engine-ready form** as the single honest leaf `gradedHilbert_fiber` (a companion
to `sectionGradedModule_fg`: it additionally records the degree-one coordinate
action from the very-ample embedding `X_s ↪ ℙ^d`, the `MvPolynomial` reshaping of
inbox `I-0109`).  The rationality of the graded Hilbert function
`Scheme.hilbertFunction`, and hence the genuineness of the Hilbert polynomial
`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (`hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually_of_projective`),
then follow as **sorry-free glue** — the `DirectSum`-decomposition bridge of
inbox `I-0109`. -/

/-- **Engine-ready packaging of Serre finiteness** for a Hilbert function
`f : ℕ → ℕ` over a field `κ`: a finitely generated (over a polynomial ring in `r`
degree-one variables) `ℕ`-graded `κ`-vector space whose `n`-th graded piece has
dimension `f n`.  This bundles exactly the data and hypotheses consumed by the
graded Hilbert–Serre rationality engine
`AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`; the engine call is
`GradedHilbert.isRatHilb`.  Geometrically `M` is the fibre section module
`⊕ₘ Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`, `grading n` is its `n`-th piece, and `gen` are the
`d+1` multiplications by the homogeneous coordinate sections. -/
structure GradedHilbert (κ : Type u) [Field κ] (f : ℕ → ℕ) where
  /-- Number of degree-one polynomial generators (`d+1` homogeneous coordinates). -/
  r : ℕ
  /-- The ambient graded `κ`-vector space. -/
  M : Type u
  [addCommGroup : AddCommGroup M]
  [module : Module κ M]
  /-- The internal `ℕ`-grading. -/
  grading : ℕ → Submodule κ M
  [decomposition : DirectSum.Decomposition grading]
  /-- Each graded piece is finite-dimensional. -/
  finiteDimensional : ∀ n, FiniteDimensional κ (grading n)
  /-- The `r` commuting degree-one endomorphisms (coordinate multiplications). -/
  gen : Fin r → Module.End κ M
  /-- The generators commute. -/
  gen_comm : ∀ i j, Commute (gen i) (gen j)
  /-- Each generator raises the grading degree by one. -/
  gen_raisesDegree : ∀ i, GradedModule.RaisesDegree grading (gen i)
  /-- The graded pieces realise the prescribed dimensions `f`. -/
  finrank_grading : ∀ n, Module.finrank κ (grading n) = f n
  /-- `M` is finite over the polynomial ring `κ[X₀,…,X_{r-1}]` acting via `gen`. -/
  finite : letI := GradedModule.polyModule gen gen_comm
    Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) M

/-- The Hilbert function packaged by a `GradedHilbert` datum is a rational Hilbert
function of order `r`: the sorry-free graded Hilbert–Serre engine call
(`AlgebraicGeometry.gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational`) followed by the
dimension identification `GradedHilbert.finrank_grading`. -/
theorem GradedHilbert.isRatHilb {κ : Type u} [Field κ] {f : ℕ → ℕ}
    (H : GradedHilbert κ f) : IsRatHilb (fun n => (f n : ℚ)) H.r := by
  haveI := H.addCommGroup
  haveI := H.module
  haveI := H.decomposition
  haveI := H.finiteDimensional
  have h := gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational H.grading H.gen H.gen_comm
    H.gen_raisesDegree H.finite
  have hfun : (fun n => (f n : ℚ))
      = fun n => (Module.finrank κ (H.grading n) : ℚ) := by
    funext n; rw [H.finrank_grading]
  rw [hfun]; exact h

variable {X S : Scheme.{0}}

/-- **Serre finiteness in engine-ready form** (companion to
`sectionGradedModule_fg`; `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`, [Nitsure] §1): for
`π : X ⟶ S` projective carrying the very ample line bundle `L` and a coherent
(finitely presented) sheaf `F`, the graded Hilbert function
`m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` at each fibre over `s : S` is packaged by
a `GradedHilbert` datum over the residue field `κ(s)`.

NAMED LEAF (`sorry`): this is `sectionGradedModule_fg` (Serre) restated in the
form the graded Hilbert–Serre rationality engine consumes.  Taking `M` to be the
fibre section module `⊕ₘ Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` and the `d+1` generators to be
the coordinate sections of the very-ample embedding `X_s ↪ ℙ^d`, its content is
the finite generation of that module over the homogeneous coordinate ring
`κ(s)[x₀,…,x_d]` — the `MvPolynomial` reshaping documented in inbox `I-0109`.
Compared with the abstract-section-ring finiteness of `sectionGradedModule_fg`,
this form additionally records the degree-one coordinate action, which is what
makes the Hilbert polynomial genuine
(`hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually_of_projective`). -/
theorem gradedHilbert_fiber (π : X ⟶ S) (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
    Nonempty (GradedHilbert (S.residueField s) (Scheme.hilbertFunction π L F s)) :=
  sorry

/-- **The graded Hilbert function of a projective morphism is a rational Hilbert
function**: from the engine-ready Serre finiteness `gradedHilbert_fiber` via
`GradedHilbert.isRatHilb`.  Inherits the single leaf `gradedHilbert_fiber` and no
other. -/
theorem hilbertFunction_isRatHilb (π : X ⟶ S) (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
    ∃ d : ℕ, IsRatHilb (fun m => (Scheme.hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) d :=
  let ⟨H⟩ := gradedHilbert_fiber π L F s hproj hF
  ⟨H.r, H.isRatHilb⟩

/-- **The Hilbert polynomial genuinely computes the Hilbert function** for a
projective morphism over a locally noetherian base: for all `m ≫ 0`,
`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial π L F s` evaluated at `m` equals
`dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction π L F s m`).
Combines `hilbertFunction_isRatHilb` with the polynomial-extraction API
`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eval_of_isRatHilb`; this is the point at which the
Hilbert polynomial ceases to be a formal `Classical.choice` and becomes the
Snapper/Hilbert polynomial of the fibre.  Inherits the single leaf
`gradedHilbert_fiber`. -/
theorem hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually_of_projective [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
    (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] (L F : X.Modules) (s : S)
    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
    ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
      (Scheme.hilbertPolynomial π L F s).eval (m : ℚ)
        = (Scheme.hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ) := by
  obtain ⟨d, hrat⟩ := hilbertFunction_isRatHilb π L F s hproj hF
  exact Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eval_of_isRatHilb π L F s hrat

end AlgebraicGeometry

data.content:
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}
[Finite generation of the section graded module]
  \label{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}
  \leanok
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule_fg}
  \uses{def:sectionGradedModule, def:sectionGradedRing, def:projective_with}
  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
  % Polynomials" (read from
  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
  %  the function
  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
  %  coherence and properness conditions."
  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); Serre's theorem on
  ample line bundles, [Hartshorne], I.7 / II.5.}
  Let \(X_s\) be projective over the field \(\kappa(s)\) carrying the very
  ample line bundle \(L_s\) (\cref{def:projective_with}: a closed immersion
  \(X_s \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}^N_{\kappa(s)}\) with
  \(L_s \cong \mathcal{O}(1)|_{X_s}\)), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a
  coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
  Then the section graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) is a
  Noetherian graded ring, and the section graded module
  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}) is a finitely
  generated graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module. In particular each component
  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a finite-dimensional
  \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space.
  (For an ample \(L_s\) the same conclusions follow by passing to a very
  ample Veronese power; the very ample form stated here is the one consumed
  by the Quot-scheme construction.)
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing, def:sectionGradedModule}
  The closed immersion \(i : X_s \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}^N_{\kappa(s)}\)
  with \(L_s \cong i^* \mathcal{O}(1)\) reduces the claims to projective
  space: pushing forward along \(i\) identifies
  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) with
  \(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^N, (i_* \mathcal{F}_s)(m))\), and \(i_* \mathcal{F}_s\)
  is coherent. By Serre's theorem the section
  graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded
  \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra (a quotient of the polynomial ring in \(N+1\)
  variables in large degrees), so it is Noetherian. For the coherent sheaf
  \(\mathcal{F}_s\), Serre's theorem yields that
  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a
  finitely generated graded module over \(R(X_s, L_s)\): the twists
  \(\mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\) are globally generated for
  \(m \gg 0\), and the finitely many generating sections in low degrees together
  with the multiplication maps generate \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) over
  \(R(X_s, L_s)\). Finite dimensionality of each component over \(\kappa(s)\)
  follows from coherence and properness (finiteness of cohomology of coherent
  sheaves on a proper scheme over a field).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Existence and uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial of a rational
  graded series]
  \label{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
  \lean{Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.HilbertPoly}).}
  Let \(F\) be a field of characteristic zero, let \(p \in F[X]\), and let
  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then there is a unique polynomial \(h \in F[X]\) such that
  for some \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) and all \(n > N\) the coefficient of \(X^n\) in the
  power-series expansion of \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d} \in F\llbracket X\rrbracket\)
  equals \(h(n)\):
  \[
    \exists!\, h \in F[X],\ \exists N,\ \forall n > N : \;
    \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1-X)^{-d} \bigr) \;=\; h(n).
  \]
  Equivalently, the eventual coefficient function of any rational power series
  with denominator a power of \(1 - X\) is, for \(n \gg 0\), given by a unique
  polynomial \(h\) (Mathlib's \(\mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d\)). The hypothesis
  \([\mathrm{CharZero}\,F]\) is satisfied by \(F = \mathbb{Q}\), which is the
  instance used to obtain \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{corollary}\leanok
[The Hilbert polynomial computes as the extracted numerator polynomial]
  \label{cor:hilbertPolynomial_eq_hilbertPoly}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_hilbertPoly}
  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:ratHilb, lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib,
    lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique}
  % ENCODING. Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_hilbertPoly: under an explicit
  % rational-series presentation of the graded Hilbert function,
  % hilbertPolynomial π L F s = Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d. Sorry-free in
  % AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean.
  Suppose there are \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and \(d, N \in \mathbb{N}\) with
  \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}(m) = [X^m]\bigl(p \cdot (1-X)^{-d}\bigr)\) for all
  \(m > N\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}). Then the Hilbert polynomial of
  \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial} is the polynomial extracted from the pair
  \((p, d)\) by \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}:
  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} = \mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d\).
\end{corollary}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib, lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique}
  \leanok
  By \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib} there is an \(N'\) with
  \((\mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d)(n) = [X^n](p\cdot(1-X)^{-d})\) for all
  \(n > N'\), so \(\mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d\) agrees with
  \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}\) at all \(m > \max(N, N')\). Both
  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) and \(\mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d\) therefore agree
  with \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}\) at all sufficiently large integers, and
  \cref{lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique} gives
  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} = \mathrm{hilbertPoly}\,p\,d\).
\end{proof}

\begin{theorem}[Hilbert polynomial from the section module]
  \label{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomialOfSectionModule}
  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:fiber_hilbert_function,
    lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,
    lem:hilbertPoly_of_isRatHilb}
  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
  % Polynomials" (read from
  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L478).
  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
  Then there is a unique polynomial \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
  such that for all \(m \gg 0\)
  \[
    \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)
    \;=\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr).
  \]
  This \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is the Hilbert polynomial of
  \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}.

  \emph{Status.} The polynomial-extraction half of this theorem is formalized:
  uniqueness (\cref{lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique}) and the extraction of the
  unique polynomial from a rational Hilbert function
  (\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_of_isRatHilb},
  \texttt{Scheme.existsUnique\_hilbertPolynomial\_of\_isRatHilb}) are
  sorry-free. The remaining input is the geometric finite-generation theorem
  \cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg} (Serre), which feeds the rationality
  hypothesis through \cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,
    lem:hilbertPoly_of_isRatHilb}
  By \cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg} the section graded module
  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded module over the
  Noetherian graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\), with finite-dimensional components and
  degree-zero part the field \(\kappa(s)\). Applying the abstract graded

  \(m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
  (\cref{def:fiber_hilbert_function}) as a rational Hilbert function of some
  order \(d\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}): there are \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\),
  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and an \(N\) with
  \(\dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
  = [X^m]\,(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d})\) for \(m > N\). The extraction lemma

text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/project-git

Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.

## The model

- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** — its git dir is
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
  this should not be a problem in general. 

So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
explicitly.

```bash
# Workspace (manifest) history:
git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
```

## Reading a project's history / diff

Run from the workspace root:

```bash
GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git
WT=<path-to-project>      # the project's `path` from the project list

git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT log --oneline -n 20
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT show <sha>
```

To see what the previous Horizon agent changed, diff from the checkpoint before
its session (use `log` to find the boundary).

## Caveats

- Every configured project is expected to have VCS enabled. If
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` is missing, report it as a workspace setup
  issue rather than treating it as normal, but work with it, it might be a new project, or a project that was cloned from the workspace's repo (hence without the vcs committed).
- You read history; you do not commit manually. Horizon checkpoints projects and
  the orchestrator writes the root integration commit.
- The bundled author identity is `Archon Horizon <archon-horizon@local>`.

39	Related: [[serretwist-cocycle-closed-serretwist-axiom-clean]] (the O(m) cocycle that de-tainted this cone), and the QuotScheme statement trap `I-0118` / Hilbert-lane `I-0109`.
40
